description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a cylindrical, bulging body and flat, circular ends. The exterior is constructed from vertical wooden staves, tightly bound by four prominent metal hoops that are riveted in place, providing structural reinforcement and a rustic appearance. The wood shows a natural, weathered grain with some darkened areas, suggesting age and exposure to the elements. The metal bands are slightly rusted and worn, adding to the authentic, utilitarian look typical of barrels used for storage or transport in urban or industrial settings. The top is fully closed, flush with the rim, and there are no visible handles or openings. The overall craftsmanship and material choices indicate it is designed for durability and heavy-duty use.
description: A weathered wooden barrel with metal hoops, bulging sides, and a closed flat top, showing signs of age and rugged use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full curvature of the barrel, with all four metal hoops and the wood grain clearly visible.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view revealing a scuffed area on the lower left side, with the metal bands and wood texture still prominent.
description_view_2: Another side view, similar to the front, with the wood and metal details consistent and no major distinguishing marks.
description_view_3: Back view, nearly identical to the front, with the same arrangement of metal hoops and wood staves.
